title: XOR N-bonacci Sequence

content.problemStatement:
Vietnamese and Bengali as well.

An NNN-bonacci sequence is an infinite sequence F1,F2,…F_1, F_2, \ldotsF1​,F2​,… such that for each integer i>Ni \gt Ni>N, FiF_iFi​ is calculated as f(Fi−1,Fi−2,…,Fi−N)f(F_{i-1}, F_{i-2}, \ldots, F_{i-N})f(Fi−1​,Fi−2​,…,Fi−N​), where fff is some function. A XOR NNN-bonacci sequence is an NNN-bonacci sequence for which f(Fi−1,Fi−2,…,Fi−N)=Fi−1⊕Fi−2⊕…⊕Fi−Nf(F_{i-1}, F_{i-2}, \ldots, F_{i-N}) = F_{i-1} \oplus F_{i−2} \oplus \ldots \oplus F_{i−N}f(Fi−1​,Fi−2​,…,Fi−N​)=Fi−1​⊕Fi−2​⊕…⊕Fi−N​, where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

Recently, Chef has found an interesting sequence S1,S2,…S_1, S_2, \ldotsS1​,S2​,…, which is obtained from prefix XORs of a XOR NNN-bonacci sequence F1,F2,…F_1, F_2, \ldotsF1​,F2​,…. Formally, for each positive integer iii, Si=F1⊕F2⊕…⊕FiS_i = F_1 \oplus F_2 \oplus \ldots \oplus F_iSi​=F1​⊕F2​⊕…⊕Fi​. You are given the first NNN elements of the sequence FFF, which uniquely determine the entire sequence SSS.

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given an index kkk and you should calculate SkS_kSk​. It is guaranteed that in each query, SkS_kSk​ does not exceed 105010^{50}1050.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5
The sum of Q over all test cases ≤ 10^5
All answers fit in a signed 64‑bit integer
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with two space‑separated integers N and Q. The next line contains N space‑separated integers F1, F2, …, FN – the first N elements of the XOR‑N‑bonacci sequence. Then follow Q lines, each containing a single integer k, the index for which the prefix XOR S_k has to be reported.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output a single line containing the value S_k, the XOR of the first k elements of the underlying XOR‑N‑bonacci sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first three terms are 0,1,2. The prefix XORs repeat every N+1=4 positions, giving S1=0, S2=1, S3=3, S4=0, then the pattern repeats. Queries retrieve the corresponding values.
With N=1 the sequence is periodic with period 2: F1=5, F2=5, …, so prefix XOR alternates between 5 and 0.
For N=2 the period is 3. Computing pref gives [0,1,3]. Queries 6,7,8 map to indices 0,1,2 respectively.

solution.approach: For a XOR‑N‑bonacci sequence we have Fi = Fi‑1 ⊕ Fi‑2 ⊕ … ⊕ Fi‑N. Let Si be the prefix XOR up to i. Using the definition one can show that Si ⊕ Si‑1 = Fi, therefore Si = S(i‑1) ⊕ Fi. Computing the first N prefix values gives an array pref[0…N] where pref[0]=0 and pref[i]=pref[i‑1]⊕Fi. A crucial observation is that the XOR of any N+1 consecutive Fi is zero, which implies that the sequence of prefix XORs repeats every N+1 positions:  S(i+N+1)=S(i)  for all i≥0. Consequently, for any query k we only need r = k mod (N+1) and answer pref[r]. The algorithm is: 1) read the first N Fi and build pref; 2) for each query compute r = k % (N+1) and output pref[r]. This runs in O(N+Q) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N + Q) per test case, O(total N + total Q) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the prefix array.
